retry_policy.principle: Retry only across finite deterministic candidates; low confidence emits evidence and recovery actions instead of autonomous re-extraction.

steps.name: candidate_start_ranking
steps.trigger: start candidates exist for requested item
steps.budget: one attempt per ranked start candidate
steps.strategy: rank non-TOC, non-dense, expected-title candidates before earlier weaker candidates

steps.name: toc_presence_filter
steps.trigger: no start candidate and TOC profile is available
steps.strategy: mark item not_present when item is absent from the TOC item list
steps.on_failure: fall through to observed sequence filter

steps.name: observed_sequence_presence_filter
steps.trigger: no start candidate and no decisive TOC absence signal
steps.strategy: mark item not_present when a long observed item sequence skips the requested item

steps.name: toc_boundary_fallback
steps.trigger: start candidate selected and TOC next item exists
steps.strategy: prefer non-TOC candidates for next items declared by the TOC
steps.on_failure: fall through to legal transition graph

steps.name: legal_graph_boundary_fallback
steps.trigger: TOC boundary is missing or weak
steps.strategy: find the earliest legal next item after the start candidate
steps.on_failure: try terminal boundary where legally allowed

steps.name: terminal_boundary_fallback
steps.trigger: terminal item or final TOC item has no legal next heading
steps.strategy: use SIGNATURES when present, otherwise EOF

steps.name: confidence_scoring
steps.strategy: score legal boundary, TOC signals, expected title, length policy, and cross-reference signals
steps.on_failure: emit warnings and recommended actions; do not autonomously retry

steps.name: recovery_action_runner
steps.budget: one deterministic runner per recommended action
steps.strategy: run page-reference extraction, internal heading selection, deferred external-source handling, or inspect-only actions
